question_text: The Moon takes approximately 29.5 days to go through all its phases (new Moon, first quarter, full Moon, last quarter, back to new Moon). If today is a new Moon, approximately how many days until the Moon is in its last quarter phase?
correct_answer: Approximately 22 days (or 22-23 days). The last quarter is three-quarters of the way through the cycle, so 3/4 × 29.5 = 22.1 days.
explanation: The lunar cycle is divided into four phases of roughly equal length. From new Moon to last quarter is three-quarters of the full 29.5-day cycle. Calculation: 3 ÷ 4 × 29.5 = 22.1 days, so about 22 days.
